key_insight: XCT-SAM improves industrial XCT defect segmentation by sequentially adapting SAM with parameter-efficient Conv-LoRA, first on alloy microstructures and then on XCT to bridge the domain gap.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical path to deploy foundation models for additive manufacturing quality inspection when labeled XCT data are scarce and defects are subtle.
method: Freeze most of SAM and train only ~4.15M parameters with Conv-LoRA adapters (r=2).
Use an intermediate alloy-microstructure adaptation step before transferring to XCT images.
Inject convolutional spatial bias into SAM to better capture microstructural defect patterns.
evidence_signal: The method is evaluated on out-of-distribution CycleGAN-XCT benchmarks and real NIST XCT scans, where it achieves the best overall IoU and Dice versus zero-shot SAM and other adapted baselines.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and relevance of the intermediate alloy-microstructure dataset, so transfer gains may not generalize equally across all XCT acquisition conditions.
paper_title: XCT-SAM: Sequential Parameter-Efficient Domain Adaptation of SAM for Industrial XCT Defect Segmentation
